rulings.Case Title: THE RASMUSSEN COMPANY, INC. VS. CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD A CALIFORNIA AGENCY
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 7, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Franchise Tax Board's demurrer to the complaint is OVERRULED. Blakemore v. Superior Court (2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 36, 53, states: A demurrer to class allegations may be sustained without leave to amend only "where it is clear that there is no reasonable possibility that the plaintiffs could establish a community of interest among the potential class members and that individual issues predominate over common questions of law and fact¿Whenever there is a reasonable possibility plaintiffs can plead a prima facie community of interest among class members, the preferred course is to defer decision on the propriety of the class action until an evidentiary hearing has been held on the appropriateness of class litigation." (internal citations and quote marks deleted.) In this case, it is not clear there is no reasonable possibility that plaintiff or a subset of plaintiffs can establish a community of interest to support class treatment. Defendant's arguments regarding individual issues predominating should be raised when and if plaintiff files a motion for class certification. Resolution of class issues at the pleading stage is most likely to occur in a mass tort action. (See Tarkington v. California Unemployment Ins. Appeals Bd. (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1511.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: STANFORD-STARTX FUND, LLC ET AL VS. MEDWHAT.COM INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To First Sets Of Discovery Requests, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 7, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF STANFORD-STARTX FUND, LLC's Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To First Sets Of Discovery Requests, And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Jeff Wohl, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. LENGTHY TENTATIVE RULING TO BE SENT TO PARTIES/COUNSEL DIRECTLY VIA EMAIL BY JPT JEFF WOHL. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jeffwohl@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STANFORD-STARTX FUND, LLC ET AL VS. MEDWHAT.COM INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 7, 2019, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANT STANFORD-STARTX FUND, LLC, CAIXA CAPITAL RISC, MAGIC STONE ALTERNATIVE INVESTMENTS, REGENT CAPITAL VENTURE LTD., STARTCAPS VENTURES, SUSAN WEINSTEIN, MARC TESSIER-LAVIGNE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-defendants Stanford-Startx Fund, LLC, Caixa Capital Risc, Magic Stone Alternative Investments, Regent Capital Venture Ltd. and Startcaps Ventures's demurrers to the second amended cross-complaint (SACC) are sustained with leave to amend. Any third amended cross-complaint (TACC) shall: (1) omit irrelevant information; (2) state ultimate facts supporting alleged causes of action in a concise manner; (3) state supporting allegations against each cross-defendant against whom a cause of action is alleged; (4) state facts supporting any fraud cause of action with particularity and plead facts as to each element of fraud; (5) state who authored what defamatory statements to support any defamation cause of action and (6) omit any cross-complainants other than pro per Arturo Devesa unless they are represented by counsel or represent themselves pro per. Cross-defendants and causes of action not pled in the SACC shall not be pled in a TACC absent grant of a noticed motion for leave to make such additions. Harris v. Wachovia Mortgage, FSB (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 1018, 1023. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for cross-defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN BORNSTEIN VS. DANIEL BORNSTEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Transfer Or Alternatively For Stay Of Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 7, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANTS DANIEL BORNSTEIN, KATHRYN QUETEL, LIANA AYRAPETYAN, DANIEL CHEUNG, VICTORIA HERNANDEZ, PATRICK WOO'S Motion To Transfer Or Alternatively For Stay Of Proceedings Defendants' two motions to transfer this case to Alameda County Superior Court are GRANTED. The case regards the break-up of a law firm owned by brothers Daniel and Jonathan Bornstein. Six cases regarding several of the same parties and the same core disputes are already pending in Alameda County, where many of the parties and witnesses live. Moreover, the Alameda County court is no doubt well-versed in the facts underlying the case from years of adjudicating the Bornsteins' disputes. Thus, the "convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by the change of venue." (CCP §397(c); see also CCP §396b.) It is noted that this motion was not brought under CCP §403. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JOE DICKINSON VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 7, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT JOHN NEMEDEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS Defendant John Nemedez's motion to quash is GRANTED. Plaintiff has the burden of demonstrating jurisdiction over Nemedez. (Mihlon v. Sup. Ct. (1985) 169 Cal.App.3d 703, 710.) But the evidence shows Nemedez is a Nevada resident and that the accident occurred in Reno. (Nemedez Dec. 1-5.) Plaintiff fails to demonstrate that Nemedez consented to California jurisdiction. Plaintiff's reliance on an Uber form "Software License and Online Services Agreement" fails to show consent in several ways. First, that agreement issued in 2014, there is no evidence Nemedez was subject to it and plaintiff's accident occurred in 2017. (Cmplt. 1.) Second, there is no competent evidence that Nemedez actually entered into the agreement or a similar document consenting to California jurisdiction. Third, the form agreement regards disputes "arising out of or in connection with this Agreement or the Uber Services." Plaintiff's car accident is unrelated to the agreement or "Uber Services." Paragraph 1.13 defines "Uber Services" as "Uber's on-demand lead generation and related services," but plaintiff's case does not involve "Uber Services;" rather, it implicates "Transportation Services" as defined in 1.12 of the agreement. Fourth, plaintiff is in effect arguing that he is an intended third-party beneficiary of the agreement between Nemedez and Uber. But 14.6 states: "There are no third party beneficiaries to this agreement." Further, for a court to exercise specific jurisdiction over a nonresident defendant, the plaintiff must show (1) "the defendant must have purposefully availed himself or herself of forum benefits or purposefully directed activities at forum residents" and (2) "the controversy must relate to or arise out of the defendant's forum-related activities." (David L. v. Sup. Ct. (2018) 29 Cal.App.5th 359, 366.) Here, the only forum-related activity was Nemedez allegedly executing a contract with Uber. Even were it to be assumed that Nemedez executed such an instrument, plaintiff's case does not arise from that activity. The nexus between the contract and the auto accident is tenuous at best. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Nemedez is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
